# Recipe Collection App

## Overview
A personal recipe management app where users can save, organize, and search through their favorite recipes for easy meal planning and cooking reference.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Recipe CRUD with ingredients list and instructions that preserve formatting
- Search functionality across recipe names, ingredients, and tags
- Image upload with proper validation and display
- Data persistence with proper relationships between recipes and their components

**Polish requirements:**
- Advanced filtering, recipe ratings, cooking timers, print-friendly layouts
- Skeleton loading states, drag-and-drop ingredient reordering

**Judgment calls:**
- Ingredient format: free-text vs. structured (amount/unit/item) — going with free-text for simplicity but structured data model for future parsing
- Search scope: full-text vs. keyword matching — implementing keyword matching with future full-text upgrade path

## Tech Stack
Rails 7 with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus), PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS.
Image storage: Active Storage with local storage (easily upgradeable to S3).
Search: PostgreSQL ILIKE queries (upgradeable to pg_search or Elasticsearch later).

## Data Model

### Recipe
- title: string (required, max 200) — recipe name for display and search
- description: text (optional, max 500) — brief summary or notes
- prep_time: integer (optional) — minutes
- cook_time: integer (optional) — minutes  
- servings: integer (optional, default: 4)
- instructions: text (required) — step-by-step cooking directions
- tags: string (optional) — comma-separated tags for categorization
- created_at, updated_at: timestamps
- Relationships: has_many Ingredients, has_one_attached image

### Ingredient
- recipe_id: integer (foreign key, required)
- amount: string (optional) — "2 cups", "1 tbsp", "to taste"
- item: string (required, max 100) — "flour", "salt", "chicken breast"
- position: integer (default: auto-increment) — for display ordering
- Relationships: belongs_to Recipe

## User Flows

### Add New Recipe
1. User clicks "Add Recipe" → sees form with title, description, prep/cook time, servings fields
2. Adds ingredients using dynamic form: click "Add Ingredient" appends amount + item fields, "Remove" deletes row with fade animation
3. Enters instructions in textarea, optionally uploads image (drag-and-drop or click-to-browse)
4. Adds optional tags (comma-separated), clicks "Save Recipe"
   - Validation: title required, at least 1 ingredient, instructions required, image under 5MB and valid format
   - On failure: highlight invalid fields with inline errors, preserve all input including uploaded image preview
   - On success: redirect to recipe detail page, flash "Recipe saved successfully"

### Search & Browse Recipes
1. User sees recipe list page with search bar at top, recipe cards in grid below
2. Types in search bar → results filter in real-time (debounced 300ms)
   - Searches across: recipe title, ingredient items, tags
   - Empty results: "No recipes found for 'chicken'. Try different keywords."
3. Clicks recipe card → navigates to recipe detail page
4. Can filter by tags using clickable tag pills above search results

### View Recipe Detail
1. Shows recipe image (or placeholder), title, description, prep/cook/total time, servings
2. Ingredients list with amounts and items, instructions as formatted text
3. Action buttons: "Edit Recipe", "Delete Recipe" (with confirmation), "Print Recipe"
4. Tags displayed as clickable pills that filter the recipe list when clicked

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **Recipe CRUD with ingredients**
   - Generate Recipe model with validations: title presence and length, instructions presence
   - Generate Ingredient model with recipe association and position for ordering
   - Recipe form with nested ingredients using Stimulus controller for dynamic add/remove rows
   - Minimum 1 ingredient enforced on submit with custom validation
   - Recipe detail page showing all fields, ingredients list, and basic action buttons
   - Edit form pre-populates all fields including existing ingredients in correct order
   - Delete with confirmation modal: "Delete '[Recipe Title]'? This cannot be undone."

2. **Image upload**
   - Add Active Storage image attachment to Recipe model
   - File upload in recipe form: drag-and-drop zone with click-to-browse fallback
   - Client-side validation: file type (jpg, png, gif, webp), size under 5MB with error messages
   - Image preview on upload before form submit, replace/remove options
   - Recipe detail page displays uploaded image with fallback placeholder for recipes without images
   - Image processing: resize to max 800px width, maintain aspect ratio, generate thumbnail variant

### Phase 2: Core Functionality  
3. **Recipe listing and search**
   - Recipe index page with responsive card grid (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
   - Each card shows: thumbnail image, title, prep+cook time, first 3 tags
   - Search bar with real-time filtering using Stimulus controller and Turbo Frames
   - Search implementation: ILIKE queries across recipe.title, ingredients.item, recipe.tags
   - Debounced search (300ms) to avoid excessive queries while typing
   - Empty states: "No recipes yet — add your first recipe!" and "No recipes found for '[query]'"
   - Results maintain grid layout, cards fade in/out smoothly during filtering

4. **Tag system**
   - Tags stored as comma-separated string in recipe.tags field
   - Recipe form includes tags input with placeholder "dinner, quick, vegetarian"
   - Tag parsing: split on commas, strip whitespace, convert to lowercase for consistency
   - Recipe detail shows tags as styled pills, clicking a tag filters recipe list to that tag
   - Tag filter page shows "Recipes tagged with '[tag]'" and clear filter option

### Phase 3: Polish
5. **Enhanced recipe display**
   - Print-friendly CSS: hide navigation, optimize for single column, page breaks between sections
   - "Print Recipe" button opens print dialog with print stylesheet applied
   - Cooking time calculations: show total time (prep + cook) when both are present
   - Servings adjustment: input field that recalculates ingredient amounts proportionally (if amounts are numeric)

6. **UI improvements**
   - Recipe cards show hover effects: slight scale and shadow increase
   - Loading states: skeleton cards while search results load, spinner on form submit
   - Responsive navigation: collapsible hamburger menu on mobile
   - Form improvements: auto-resize textarea for instructions, tab order optimization
   - Ingredient list: drag handles for reordering (updates position field via AJAX)

## Validation Rules
- Recipe title: required, 1-200 characters
- Recipe instructions: required, minimum 10 characters
- At least 1 ingredient per recipe (custom validation)
- Ingredient item: required, 1-100 characters
- Image upload: jpg/png/gif/webp only, max 5MB
- Tags: optional, max 200 characters total

## Error Handling
- Form validation errors display inline below each field with red styling
- File upload errors show immediately on file selection before form submit
- Search errors (if database unavailable): "Search temporarily unavailable. Please try again."
- Image processing failures: save recipe without image, show warning "Image could not be processed"
- Network errors on form submit: preserve all input, show retry option

## UI/UX Notes
- Mobile-first responsive design with breakpoints at 768px (tablet) and 1024px (desktop)
- Recipe cards use 4:3 aspect ratio for consistent grid layout
- Color scheme: warm colors (oranges, browns) for food-focused aesthetic
- Typography: readable serif font for recipe content, sans-serif for UI elements
- Accessibility: proper heading hierarchy, alt text for images, keyboard navigation for all interactive elements

## Assumptions
- Single-user app (no authentication needed, but database ready for user_id scoping)
- Ingredients stored as free-text for flexibility, not structured amount/unit/item parsing
- Local image storage acceptable for initial version (no CDN needed)
- Search is keyword-based, not full-text semantic search
- Tags are simple comma-separated strings, not a separate Tag model with relationships
- Recipe sharing/export not needed in v1
- No recipe ratings or favorites system in v1

## Out of Scope
- User authentication and multi-user support
- Recipe sharing or public recipe discovery
- Meal planning or shopping list generation
- Recipe import from URLs or other apps
- Nutritional information calculation
- Recipe ratings or review system
- Advanced search with filters (cuisine type, dietary restrictions)

## Future Considerations
- **Multi-user support**: Add User model, authentication, scope all recipes by user_id
- **Recipe sharing**: Public recipe URLs, recipe export to PDF/email
- **Structured ingredients**: Parse amounts/units for better serving size adjustment and shopping lists
- **Advanced search**: Full-text search with PostgreSQL or Elasticsearch, filter by prep time, dietary tags
- **Recipe import**: Parse recipes from popular cooking websites
- **Meal planning**: Weekly meal planner with drag-and-drop recipes
- **Mobile app**: React Native or PWA version for kitchen use
- **Recipe collections**: Group recipes into cookbooks or meal categories

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 4/5
- Specificity: 5/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 5/5
- Translation Quality: 4/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Initially missed the Ingredient position field needed for display ordering — added it to data model
- Recipe deletion flow didn't specify confirmation pattern — added modal with recipe title
- Search empty state was generic — made it show the actual search query for better UX
- Image upload validation was only server-side — added client-side validation for immediate feedback
- Tag filtering didn't have a clear filter option — added "clear filter" functionality

**Remaining considerations:**
- Should ingredient amounts be parsed into structured data (amount/unit/item) for better serving adjustment, or keep as free-text for user flexibility?
- Is real-time search filtering worth the complexity, or would search-on-submit be sufficient?
- Should recipes without images show a generic food placeholder or remain empty?

**Questions for Consideration:**
1. Do you want users to be able to rate recipes (1-5 stars) or mark favorites for easier finding?
2. Should the app support recipe categories/cuisines beyond just tags (like dropdown filters)?
3. Would you like a shopping list feature that combines ingredients from multiple selected recipes?
4. Do you need recipe scaling (automatically adjust ingredient amounts for different serving sizes)?
5. Should there be a "recently viewed" or "recently added" section on the main page?

# Handmade Crafts Marketplace

## Overview
A two-sided marketplace connecting craft makers with buyers, featuring product listings, secure payments, order management, and a review system to build seller reputation.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Dual user roles (seller/buyer) with different permissions and dashboards
- Payment processing with seller payouts (minus platform fee)
- Order lifecycle (placed → paid → shipped → delivered → reviewed)
- Product listing with photos and inventory tracking
- Review system tied to completed purchases only

**Polish requirements:**
- Advanced search/filtering, seller analytics dashboard, email notifications
- Image optimization, SEO-friendly URLs, social sharing

**Judgment calls:**
- Platform fee: default to 5% but make configurable via admin settings
- Payment timing: hold funds until "delivered" status, then auto-release to seller after 7 days
- Shipping: sellers set their own rates and methods (no integrated shipping calculator in v1)

## Tech Stack
Rails 7 with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus), PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS, Stripe for payments, AWS S3 for image storage, ActionMailer with SendGrid for transactional emails.

## Data Model

### User
- email: string (required, unique, valid format)
- password_digest: string (bcrypt hashed)
- first_name: string (required, max 50)
- last_name: string (required, max 50)
- role: enum [buyer, seller, admin] (default: buyer)
- stripe_account_id: string (nullable, for seller payouts)
- created_at, updated_at: timestamps
- Relationships: has_many Products (if seller), has_many Orders (as buyer), has_many Reviews

### Product
- title: string (required, max 100)
- description: text (required, max 2000)
- price: decimal (required, > 0, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- quantity_available: integer (required, >= 0)
- category: enum [jewelry, pottery, textiles, woodwork, art, other]
- status: enum [draft, active, sold_out, archived] (default: draft)
- shipping_cost: decimal (required, >= 0, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- processing_time_days: integer (required, 1-30) — how long to make/prepare item
- created_at, updated_at: timestamps
- Relationships: belongs_to User (seller), has_many ProductImages, has_many OrderItems, has_many Reviews

### ProductImage
- image_url: string (required, S3 URL)
- alt_text: string (required, max 200, for accessibility)
- display_order: integer (required, for sorting)
- Relationships: belongs_to Product

### Order
- order_number: string (auto-generated, unique, format: ORD-YYYYMMDD-XXXX)
- status: enum [pending, paid, shipped, delivered, cancelled] (default: pending)
- subtotal: decimal (sum of order_items.price * quantity)
- shipping_total: decimal (sum of shipping costs)
- platform_fee: decimal (subtotal * platform_fee_percentage)
- total: decimal (subtotal + shipping_total)
- stripe_payment_intent_id: string (for payment tracking)
- shipping_address: jsonb (street, city, state, zip, country)
- shipped_at: datetime (nullable)
- delivered_at: datetime (nullable)
- created_at, updated_at: timestamps
- Relationships: belongs_to User (buyer), has_many OrderItems

### OrderItem
- quantity: integer (required, > 0)
- price: decimal (product price at time of purchase, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- Relationships: belongs_to Order, belongs_to Product

### Review
- rating: integer (required, 1-5)
- comment: text (optional, max 1000)
- created_at, updated_at: timestamps
- Relationships: belongs_to User (reviewer), belongs_to Product, belongs_to Order (ensures review tied to purchase)

## User Flows

### Seller Registration & Setup
1. User clicks "Sell on [Platform]" → registration form (email, password, first/last name, role auto-set to seller)
2. Email verification required before account activation
3. After verification, redirect to seller onboarding: "Set up your shop" → Stripe Connect account creation
4. Stripe OAuth flow → on success: stripe_account_id saved, redirect to "Create your first product"

### Product Listing Creation
1. Seller clicks "Add Product" → form with title, description, price, quantity, category, shipping cost, processing time
2. Image upload: drag-and-drop or click-to-browse, max 5 images, 5MB each, auto-resize to 800px width
3. Preview mode shows how listing will appear to buyers
4. Save as draft or publish immediately (status: draft/active)
5. Validation: all fields required except description can be shorter, price > 0, quantity >= 0, at least 1 image
6. On success: redirect to product detail page with flash "Product created successfully"

### Buyer Purchase Flow
1. Buyer browses products → clicks product → sees detail page with images, description, price, shipping cost, seller info
2. Clicks "Add to Cart" → quantity selector (max: available quantity), updates cart count in header
3. Cart page: review items, quantities, shipping per seller (group by seller), total calculation
4. Checkout: shipping address form, payment method (Stripe Elements), order summary
5. Payment processing: create Stripe PaymentIntent, on success → order status: paid, inventory decremented
6. Confirmation page with order number, email receipt sent

### Order Fulfillment
1. Seller receives email notification of new order with buyer details and shipping address
2. Seller dashboard shows pending orders → clicks order → sees items, shipping address, "Mark as Shipped" button
3. Mark as shipped: optional tracking number field, updates status to shipped, buyer gets email notification
4. Buyer can mark as delivered (or auto-delivered after 14 days from shipped date)
5. 7 days after delivered: funds auto-release to seller's Stripe account (minus platform fee)

### Review System
1. After order status = delivered, buyer sees "Leave Review" button on order history page
2. Review form: 1-5 star rating (required), optional comment, per product in the order
3. Reviews appear on product page, sorted by newest first
4. Seller can respond to reviews (optional feature for Phase 3)

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User authentication & roles**
   - Devise gem for auth with email/password, email confirmation required
   - User model with role enum, registration form captures first/last name
   - Role-based navigation: sellers see "My Products" and "Orders", buyers see "My Orders"
   - Login/logout with redirect to appropriate dashboard based on role

2. **Product management (seller side)**
   - Product CRUD with all fields, category dropdown, price/quantity validation (price > 0, quantity >= 0)
   - Image upload to S3 using Active Storage, max 5 images per product, client-side preview before upload
   - Image reordering with drag-and-drop using Stimulus, alt text required for accessibility
   - Product status management: draft products not visible to buyers, active products appear in listings
   - Seller product index: shows all their products with status badges, quick actions (edit, archive, duplicate)

3. **Product browsing (buyer side)**
   - Public product index: grid layout, shows active products only, pagination (20 per page)
   - Product detail page: image carousel, description, price, shipping cost, seller name, "Add to Cart" button
   - Basic search: title and description text search with results highlighting
   - Category filtering: sidebar with category counts, URL-based (/products?category=jewelry)

### Phase 2: Core Commerce
4. **Shopping cart**
   - Session-based cart (no login required to add items), cart count in header updates via Turbo
   - Cart page: item list with thumbnails, quantity adjustment (with stock validation), remove items
   - Shipping calculation: group items by seller, show shipping cost per seller, total calculation
   - Empty cart state: "Your cart is empty" with link to browse products

5. **Stripe payment integration**
   - Stripe Connect for seller accounts: OAuth flow during seller onboarding, store stripe_account_id
   - Checkout page: shipping address form (required fields), Stripe Elements for payment
   - Payment processing: create PaymentIntent, handle 3D Secure, inventory decrement on successful payment
   - Order confirmation: display order number, email receipt with order details and seller contact info

6. **Order management**
   - Order model with status lifecycle, order items capture product price at purchase time
   - Seller order dashboard: pending orders with buyer shipping details, "Mark as Shipped" action
   - Buyer order history: order status tracking, order details with product info and seller contact
   - Email notifications: order confirmation (buyer), new order (seller), shipped notification (buyer)

### Phase 3: Trust & Polish
7. **Review system**
   - Reviews only allowed for delivered orders, one review per product per order
   - Review form: star rating (required), comment (optional, 1000 char max), spam prevention via rate limiting
   - Product page review display: average rating, individual reviews with reviewer name and date
   - Review moderation: admin can hide inappropriate reviews, sellers notified of new reviews

8. **Seller payouts**
   - Funds held until order delivered + 7 days, automatic transfer to seller's Stripe account
   - Platform fee calculation (5% default, configurable), fee deducted before payout
   - Seller earnings dashboard: pending/available balance, payout history, transaction details

9. **Enhanced search & discovery**
   - Advanced filtering: price range, category, rating, location (if seller provides)
   - Search improvements: autocomplete, search suggestions, "no results" state with suggested alternatives
   - Product recommendations: "Similar items" on product pages, "Recently viewed" for logged-in users

## Validation Rules
- User email: unique, valid format, confirmed before account activation
- Product price: > 0, max 2 decimal places
- Product quantity: >= 0, integer only
- Product images: max 5 per product, 5MB each, JPEG/PNG only
- Order quantity: <= available stock at time of purchase
- Review rating: 1-5 integer, required
- Shipping address: all fields required (street, city, state, zip, country)

## Error Handling
- Payment failures: preserve cart contents, show specific error message, retry option
- Out of stock: remove item from cart with notification, suggest similar products
- Image upload failures: show per-file error messages, allow retry without losing other uploads
- Form validation: inline error messages on blur, disable submit until valid, preserve input on server errors
- 404 errors: custom page with search bar and popular products
- 500 errors: friendly error page with support contact, error logged for admin review

## UI/UX Notes
- Responsive design: mobile-first approach, product grid collapses to single column on mobile
- Image handling: lazy loading for product grids, zoom functionality on product detail pages
- Loading states: skeleton screens for product grids, spinners for form submissions, progress bars for image uploads
- Accessibility: proper heading hierarchy, alt text for all images, keyboard navigation, focus management in modals
- Color coding: order status badges (gray=pending, blue=paid, orange=shipped, green=delivered)

## Assumptions
- Platform takes 5% fee from each sale (configurable via admin settings)
- Sellers handle their own shipping and set their own rates
- No integrated shipping calculator (sellers estimate shipping costs)
- Single currency (USD) with standard formatting
- Orders auto-mark as delivered 14 days after shipped if buyer doesn't confirm
- Funds auto-release to sellers 7 days after delivery
- No subscription fees for sellers (revenue from transaction fees only)
- Reviews are public and cannot be deleted by sellers (only hidden by admin for policy violations)
- No messaging system between buyers and sellers in v1
- No inventory alerts/low stock warnings in v1

## Out of Scope
- Multi-currency support
- International shipping calculations
- Seller subscription plans
- Advanced analytics/reporting
- Bulk product import/export
- Seller messaging system
- Wishlist/favorites functionality
- Social media integration
- Mobile app (web-responsive only)

## Future Considerations
- **Seller messaging**: Direct communication between buyers and sellers for custom orders
- **Advanced shipping**: Integration with shipping APIs for real-time rate calculation
- **Seller analytics**: Traffic, conversion rates, popular products, seasonal trends
- **Promotional tools**: Discount codes, sales events, featured listings
- **International expansion**: Multi-currency, localized payment methods, tax calculation
- **Mobile apps**: Native iOS/Android apps with push notifications
- **Seller verification**: Identity verification, business license validation for professional sellers
- **Advanced search**: AI-powered recommendations, visual search, saved searches
- **Inventory management**: Low stock alerts, automatic draft when sold out, bulk editing tools

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 4/5
- Specificity: 5/5
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 5/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Initially missed the platform fee calculation in the Order model — added platform_fee field and calculation logic
- Payment flow didn't specify inventory decrement timing — clarified it happens on successful payment, not order creation
- Review system needed constraint to prevent multiple reviews per product per order — added order relationship to Review model
- Seller payout timing was vague — specified 7-day hold after delivery with automatic release
- Missing email notifications for key order status changes — added to order management feature

**Remaining considerations:**
- Should sellers be able to offer multiple shipping options per product, or is single shipping cost sufficient for v1?
- Do you want buyer protection (dispute resolution) beyond the 7-day hold period?
- Should there be seller verification requirements before they can receive payouts?

**Questions for Consideration:**
1. What should the platform fee percentage be? (I assumed 5% but this significantly impacts revenue)
2. Do you want to allow guest checkout, or require account creation for purchases?
3. Should sellers be able to create product variations (size, color) or keep it simple with separate listings?
4. What's your policy on refunds and returns? Should this be seller-managed or platform-managed?
5. Do you need any content moderation for product listings beyond basic spam prevention?

I understand you want to add a book reservation system to your existing Rails bookstore app, where customers can reserve books online and then pick them up at the physical store.

Here's my initial draft spec based on what you've described:

---

# Book Reservation Feature

## Overview
Add in-store pickup functionality to existing bookstore Rails app. Customers can reserve available books online and pick them up at the physical store within a specified timeframe.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Inventory tracking integration with existing book stock
- Reservation expiration to prevent indefinite holds on inventory
- Staff interface to process pickups and manage reservations

**Polish requirements:**
- Email notifications for reservation confirmations and reminders
- Customer reservation history and management

**Judgment calls:**
- Reservation hold period: defaulting to 7 days but should be configurable
- Whether to allow partial pickups for multi-book reservations
- Integration approach with existing User/Customer and Book models

## Tech Stack
Rails 7 (existing), integrate with current authentication system, existing database.

## Data Model Changes

### Reservation (new table)
- customer_id: integer (foreign key to existing Customer/User model)
- status: enum [pending, ready, picked_up, expired, cancelled] (default: pending)
- reservation_date: datetime (default: current timestamp)
- expiry_date: datetime (reservation_date + configurable hold period)
- pickup_notes: text (optional, staff notes)
- created_at, updated_at: datetime

### ReservationItem (new table)
- reservation_id: integer (foreign key)
- book_id: integer (foreign key to existing Book model)
- quantity: integer (default: 1, must be > 0)
- status: enum [reserved, picked_up, cancelled] (default: reserved)

### Book model updates (existing table)
- Add: reserved_quantity: integer (default: 0) — tracks how many copies are currently reserved
- Computed: available_quantity = stock_quantity - reserved_quantity

## User Flows

### Customer Reserves Books
1. Customer browses books, sees "Reserve for Pickup" button only on books with available_quantity > 0
2. Clicks reserve → modal/page shows reservation form with quantity selector (max: available_quantity)
3. Can add multiple books to reservation before submitting
4. Submits reservation → system validates stock, creates reservation with status=pending
5. Confirmation page shows reservation details and pickup instructions

### Staff Processes Pickup
1. Staff accesses reservations dashboard, sees list filtered by status=pending
2. Customer arrives, staff searches by customer name/email or reservation ID
3. Staff marks individual items as picked up or cancels items if unavailable
4. When all items processed, reservation status updates to picked_up

### System Handles Expiration
1. Daily background job finds reservations where expiry_date < current_date and status=pending
2. Updates status to expired, releases reserved inventory back to available stock

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Core Reservation System
1. **Reservation models and associations**
   - Generate Reservation and ReservationItem models with validations
   - Add reserved_quantity to Book model with database migration
   - Set up associations: Customer has_many reservations, Reservation has_many reservation_items
   - Validation: reservation must have at least 1 item, quantity must not exceed available stock

2. **Customer reservation interface**

   - Form validation: check stock availability on submit, show inline errors for out-of-stock items
   - Confirmation page: reservation number, items reserved, pickup deadline, store hours/location

### Phase 2: Staff Management
3. **Staff reservation dashboard**
   - New admin route: /admin/reservations with filterable list (pending, ready, expired)

### Phase 3: Automation & Polish
5. **Expiration handling**

## Assumptions
- Existing Rails app has Customer/User authentication system

---

# Book Reservation Feature

## Overview
Add in-store pickup functionality to existing Rails bookstore app. Customers can reserve available books online through the catalog and pick them up at the physical store within 7 days.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Inventory tracking integration with existing Book stock management
- Reservation expiration system to prevent indefinite holds on inventory
- Staff interface integrated with existing admin patterns

**Polish requirements:**
- Email notifications for confirmations and reminders
- Hotwire-powered real-time stock updates on catalog page

**Judgment calls:**
- 7-day hold period (configurable via Rails config)
- Staff can process partial pickups (some items unavailable at pickup time)
- Reservations don't require payment upfront

## Tech Stack
Rails 7 with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus), PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS, Devise authentication (existing).

## Data Model Changes

### Reservation (new table)
```ruby
# app/models/reservation.rb
class Reservation < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :customer
  has_many :reservation_items, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :books, through: :reservation_items
  
  enum status: { pending: 0, ready: 1, picked_up: 2, expired: 3, cancelled: 4 }
  
  validates :expiry_date, presence: true
  validates :reservation_items, presence: true
  
  scope :active, -> { where(status: [:pending, :ready]) }
  scope :expired_today, -> { where('expiry_date < ? AND status = ?', Date.current, statuses[:pending]) }
end
```

Fields:
- customer_id: references (foreign key, required)
- status: integer (enum: pending=0, ready=1, picked_up=2, expired=3, cancelled=4, default: pending)
- reservation_date: datetime (default: current timestamp)
- expiry_date: datetime (calculated: reservation_date + 7.days)
- pickup_notes: text (optional, staff notes)
- reservation_number: string (unique, auto-generated: "RES-#{SecureRandom.hex(4).upcase}")

### ReservationItem (new table)
```ruby
# app/models/reservation_item.rb
class ReservationItem < ApplicationRecord
  belongs_to :reservation
  belongs_to :book
  
  enum status: { reserved: 0, picked_up: 1, cancelled: 2 }
  
  validates :quantity, presence: true, numericality: { greater_than: 0 }
  validate :quantity_available
  
  private
  
  def quantity_available
    return unless book && quantity
    
    if quantity > book.available_for_reservation
      errors.add(:quantity, "only #{book.available_for_reservation} copies available")
    end
  end
end
```

Fields:
- reservation_id: references (foreign key, required)
- book_id: references (foreign key, required)
- quantity: integer (required, > 0, default: 1)
- status: integer (enum: reserved=0, picked_up=1, cancelled=2, default: reserved)

### Book model updates (existing table)
Add migration to add:
- reserved_quantity: integer (default: 0, not null)

Update Book model:
```ruby
# Add to existing app/models/book.rb
has_many :reservation_items, dependent: :restrict_with_error
has_many :reservations, through: :reservation_items

def available_for_reservation
  [stock_quantity - reserved_quantity, 0].max
end

def reserve!(quantity)
  increment!(:reserved_quantity, quantity)
end

def release_reservation!(quantity)
  decrement!(:reserved_quantity, quantity)
end
```

## User Flows

### Customer Creates Reservation
1. Customer browses catalog page → sees "Reserve for Pickup" button on books with available_for_reservation > 0
2. Clicks "Reserve" → Turbo modal opens with quantity selector (max: available_for_reservation)
3. Selects quantity → "Add to Reservation" button adds item to session-based reservation cart
4. Can continue browsing and adding more books to same reservation
5. Clicks "Review Reservation" (cart icon with count) → reservation summary page
6. Reviews items, quantities, pickup deadline → clicks "Confirm Reservation"
7. System validates stock, creates reservation, updates book.reserved_quantity
8. Turbo redirects to confirmation page with reservation number and pickup instructions

### Customer Views Reservations
1. Customer dashboard shows "My Reservations" section with active reservations
2. Each reservation shows: reservation number, items, status, expiry date
3. "Cancel Reservation" button (Turbo confirm dialog) for pending reservations only

### Staff Processes Pickup
1. Staff accesses /admin/reservations → filterable table (pending/ready/all)
2. Search by customer name, email, or reservation number (Stimulus search controller)
3. Clicks reservation → detail page with customer info and item checklist
4. For each item: checkbox "Picked up" + optional notes field
5. "Complete Pickup" button → Turbo updates reservation status, releases unreserved items

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Core Models & Associations
1. **Database setup**
   - Generate Reservation model with fields and validations
   - Generate ReservationItem model with associations and quantity validation
   - Add reserved_quantity migration to books table with index
   - Add Book model methods: available_for_reservation, reserve!, release_reservation!
   - Seed admin user if not exists for testing staff interface

2. **Reservation cart system**
   - Session-based reservation cart (before customer commits to actual reservation)
   - Stimulus controller for "Add to Reservation" buttons on catalog
   - Cart icon in header with item count (updates via Turbo Stream)
   - Cart modal/page showing selected books with quantity controls and remove buttons

### Phase 2: Customer Interface
3. **Catalog integration**
   - Add "Reserve for Pickup" button to book cards/rows (visible only when available_for_reservation > 0)
   - Button disabled with "Out of Stock" when available_for_reservation = 0
   - Stimulus controller handles quantity selection modal with real-time availability check
   - Turbo Stream updates to reflect stock changes without page refresh

4. **Reservation creation flow**
   - Reservation summary page: review cart items, see pickup deadline (7 days from now)
   - Form submission creates Reservation + ReservationItems in transaction
   - On success: update book.reserved_quantity, clear cart, Turbo redirect to confirmation
   - On failure: show validation errors inline, preserve cart contents
   - Confirmation page: reservation number, pickup instructions, "Add to Calendar" link

5. **Customer reservation management**
   - Add "My Reservations" section to existing customer dashboard
   - Table showing: reservation number, items (truncated list), status badge, expiry date
   - "View Details" link → full reservation page with all items and pickup status
   - "Cancel Reservation" button (pending only) → Turbo confirm → releases inventory

### Phase 3: Staff Interface
6. **Admin reservations dashboard**
   - New route: /admin/reservations with Ransack search (customer name, email, reservation number)
   - Filterable table: status tabs (All/Pending/Ready/Expired) with counts
   - Table columns: reservation number, customer, items count, status, expiry date, actions
   - Responsive: collapses to cards on mobile

7. **Pickup processing**
   - Reservation detail page: customer contact info, reservation items with checkboxes
   - Stimulus controller for item-level pickup: check item → disable checkbox, show "Picked up" badge
   - Notes textarea per item for staff comments (damaged, substitution, etc.)
   - "Complete Pickup" button appears when at least one item checked → updates reservation status
   - Turbo Stream updates: real-time status changes, inventory released for unchecked items

### Phase 4: Automation & Polish
8. **Background job system**
   - Daily cron job (whenever gem): find expired reservations, update status, release inventory
   - Job logs for monitoring: "Expired 5 reservations, released 12 books to stock"
   - Reservation expiry email 1 day before expiration (optional, can be disabled)

9. **Email notifications**
   - ReservationMailer: confirmation email with pickup details and calendar attachment
   - Expiry reminder email 24 hours before deadline
   - Pickup confirmation email when reservation completed
   - All emails use existing ActionMailer configuration and templates match site design

10. **Real-time updates**
    - Turbo Stream broadcasts when reservations created/cancelled → update catalog availability
    - Staff dashboard auto-refreshes pickup counts via Turbo Stream
    - Customer dashboard shows live reservation status updates

## Validation Rules
- Reservation must have at least 1 reservation_item
- ReservationItem quantity must not exceed book.available_for_reservation at creation time
- Cannot modify reservation after creation (cancel only)
- Staff can only mark items as picked up, not increase quantities

## Error Handling
- Stock validation failure: show inline error "Only X copies available", preserve other cart items
- Reservation creation failure: transaction rollback, show field-specific errors, preserve cart
- Concurrent reservation conflict: "This book was just reserved by another customer" with refresh option
- Background job failures: log errors, send admin notification, retry with exponential backoff

## UI/UX Notes
- Reserve buttons use Tailwind primary color scheme matching existing site
- Reservation status badges: pending (yellow), ready (blue), picked_up (green), expired (red), cancelled (gray)
- Modal overlays use existing site modal patterns with proper focus trapping
- Mobile-first responsive: reservation tables collapse to card layout under 768px
- Loading states: disable buttons with spinner during Turbo requests
- Empty states: "No reservations yet" with link to catalog

## Assumptions
- 7-day hold period (configurable via Rails.application.config.reservation_hold_days)
- Single store location for pickup
- No payment required for reservations
- Staff access uses existing admin authentication (admin role on Customer model or separate admin system)
- Email delivery configured (existing ActionMailer setup)
- Book model has stock_quantity field for inventory tracking
- Customers can cancel their own pending reservations
- Staff can process partial pickups (mark some items picked up, cancel others)

## Out of Scope
- Payment processing for reservations
- Multi-location pickup
- Reservation modifications (quantity changes, item swaps)
- SMS notifications
- Reservation transfers between customers

## Future Considerations
- Reservation modification requests (extend deadline, change quantities)
- SMS notifications via Twilio integration
- Analytics dashboard: reservation conversion rates, popular pickup times
- Integration with POS system for seamless checkout
- Reservation deposits for high-value items
- Bulk reservation processing for staff efficiency

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 5/5
- Specificity: 5/5  
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 5/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Added transaction handling for reservation creation to prevent partial failures
- Specified Turbo Stream patterns for real-time updates matching Rails 7 + Hotwire conventions
- Added proper validation for concurrent stock conflicts
- Included mobile-responsive patterns for admin interface
- Added session-based cart system before reservation commitment

**Remaining considerations:**
- Should staff have ability to extend reservation deadlines on customer request?
- Do you want reservation analytics/reporting in the initial build?
- Should there be a maximum number of books per reservation to prevent abuse?

# Invoice CRUD with Status Machine

## Overview
A Rails application for managing invoices with line items, status workflow management, and PDF generation capabilities.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Status machine transitions must be enforced (draft → sent → paid/overdue only)
- Line item calculations must be accurate and update totals in real-time
- PDF generation must include all invoice data and be downloadable
- Status changes must be auditable and irreversible once sent

**Polish requirements:**
- Real-time total updates via Stimulus, skeleton loading states, toast notifications
- Responsive design for mobile invoice viewing

**Judgment calls:**
- Invoice numbering: auto-increment with INV- prefix (customizable later)
- Overdue detection: background job vs. computed property (using background job for performance)
- PDF styling: basic professional layout (enhanced styling can be added later)

## Tech Stack
Rails 7 with Hotwire (Turbo + Stimulus), PostgreSQL, Tailwind CSS for styling.
PDF generation: Prawn gem for layout control.
Background jobs: Sidekiq for overdue detection.
State machine: AASM gem for status transitions.

## Data Model

### Invoice
- number: string (unique, auto-generated) — display identifier (INV-0001, INV-0002)
- status: string (enum via AASM: draft, sent, paid, overdue) — workflow state
- issue_date: date (set when status changes to sent)
- due_date: date (required, must be >= issue_date when sent)
- client_name: string (required, max 100) — billing recipient
- client_email: string (required, valid email format) — for PDF delivery
- client_address: text (optional) — billing address block
- notes: text (optional) — additional terms or notes
- subtotal: decimal (computed from line items, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- tax_rate: decimal (default: 0, precision: 5, scale: 4) — percentage as decimal
- tax_amount: decimal (computed: subtotal * tax_rate, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- total: decimal (computed: subtotal + tax_amount, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- created_at: datetime
- updated_at: datetime
- Relationships: has_many LineItems (dependent: destroy)

### LineItem
- description: string (required, max 200) — service/product description
- quantity: decimal (required, > 0, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- unit_price: decimal (required, >= 0, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- amount: decimal (computed: quantity * unit_price, precision: 10, scale: 2)
- position: integer (for ordering, auto-assigned)
- Relationships: belongs_to Invoice

## User Flows

### Create Invoice
1. User clicks "New Invoice" → sees form with client fields and empty line items section
2. Fills client information (name required, email required with format validation, address optional)
3. Adds line items using "Add Line Item" button → new row appears with description, quantity, unit_price fields
4. As user types in quantity/unit_price → Stimulus controller recalculates line totals and invoice subtotal/total in real-time
5. Sets due_date (defaults to 30 days from today, must be future date)
6. Clicks "Save Draft" → validation runs → on success: redirect to invoice show page with flash "Invoice saved as draft" → on failure: highlight invalid fields with inline errors, preserve all input

### Send Invoice
1. From invoice show page (draft status only) → user clicks "Send Invoice" button
2. Confirmation modal appears: "Send invoice INV-0042 to client@email.com? This will mark it as sent and set the issue date."
3. On confirm → AASM transition draft → sent, issue_date set to today, button disabled with spinner
4. On success → page refreshes, status badge updates to "Sent", button replaced with "Mark as Paid", flash "Invoice sent successfully"
5. On failure → modal closes, error flash appears with transition failure reason

### Mark as Paid
1. From invoice show page (sent or overdue status) → user clicks "Mark as Paid" button
2. Confirmation modal: "Mark Invoice INV-0042 as paid? This cannot be undone."
3. On confirm → AASM transition to paid, button disabled with spinner
4. On success → status badge updates to "Paid", button disappears, flash "Invoice marked as paid"

### Overdue Detection (Automated)
1. Daily background job runs at 6 AM
2. Finds all invoices where status = 'sent' AND due_date < Date.current
3. Transitions each to 'overdue' status via AASM
4. Logs transition for audit trail

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **Invoice model with AASM**
   - Generate Invoice model with all fields and validations
   - Add AASM gem, configure states: draft (initial), sent, paid, overdue
   - Define transitions: draft → sent, sent → paid, sent → overdue, overdue → paid
   - Add before_transition callback: draft → sent sets issue_date to Date.current
   - Validation: due_date must be present and >= issue_date (only when sent/overdue)
   - Add auto-incrementing invoice number generation in before_create callback

2. **LineItem model with calculations**
   - Generate LineItem model with validations (quantity > 0, unit_price >= 0)
   - Add position field with auto-assignment (max position + 1)
   - Add amount calculation method: quantity * unit_price
   - Add Invoice callbacks: after_save/destroy on line_items → recalculate invoice totals
   - Invoice total calculation methods: subtotal (sum line_items.amount), tax_amount (subtotal * tax_rate), total (subtotal + tax_amount)

### Phase 2: CRUD Interface
3. **Invoice index and show pages**
   - Index page: table with columns (number, client_name, status badge, due_date, total, actions)
   - Status badges: color-coded (gray=draft, blue=sent, red=overdue, green=paid)
   - Filter tabs: All, Draft, Sent, Overdue, Paid with counts
   - Show page: invoice header (number, status, dates), client info block, line items table, totals section
   - Empty state on index: "No invoices yet. Create your first invoice to get started."

4. **Invoice form with dynamic line items**
   - New/edit form with client fields at top, line items section below
   - Line items: dynamic rows using Stimulus controller for add/remove functionality
   - "Add Line Item" button appends new row with fade-in animation
   - Each row: description (text input), quantity (number input, step=0.01), unit_price (number input, step=0.01), amount (read-only, calculated), remove button (×)
   - Remove button: fade-out animation, minimum 1 line item enforced
   - Running totals section: subtotal, tax (if tax_rate > 0), total - updates live via Stimulus
   - Form validation: client_name required, client_email required with format check, at least 1 line item, all line item fields required
   - Submit button: "Save Draft" (always available), "Save & Send" (only on new invoices)

### Phase 3: Status Management
5. **Status transition buttons and confirmations**
   - Show page: status-appropriate action buttons (Send Invoice, Mark as Paid)
   - Send Invoice: only visible on draft status, triggers confirmation modal with client email
   - Mark as Paid: visible on sent/overdue status, triggers confirmation modal with invoice number
   - Confirmation modals: Turbo-powered, focus trap, escape to close, backdrop click to close
   - Button states during transitions: disabled + spinner, success feedback via flash + page refresh
   - Error handling: display AASM transition errors in flash messages

6. **Overdue detection job**
   - Create OverdueInvoicesJob with daily schedule (whenever gem or cron)
   - Job logic: Invoice.where(status: 'sent').where('due_date < ?', Date.current).find_each { |i| i.mark_overdue! }
   - Add mark_overdue! method to Invoice model using AASM transition
   - Job logging: Rails.logger.info with count of invoices marked overdue
   - Error handling: rescue and log individual invoice transition failures, continue processing

### Phase 4: PDF Generation
7. **PDF generation with Prawn**
   - Add Prawn gem to Gemfile
   - Create InvoicePdf service class with generate method
   - PDF layout: header with "INVOICE" title and invoice number, client address block, line items table with headers, totals section, notes section if present
   - Styling: professional fonts, proper spacing, borders for table, right-aligned numbers
   - "Download PDF" button on invoice show page → streams PDF with filename "Invoice-#{number}.pdf"
   - Loading state: disable button + show "Generating..." text while PDF renders
   - Error handling: rescue PDF generation errors, show flash message "PDF generation failed, please try again"

### Phase 5: Polish & UX
8. **Responsive design and mobile optimization**
   - Invoice index: table collapses to card layout on mobile (< 768px)
   - Invoice form: single column layout on mobile, line items stack vertically
   - Show page: client info and totals stack on mobile
   - Touch-friendly button sizes (min 44px), proper spacing for mobile interaction

9. **Enhanced interactions and feedback**
   - Toast notifications for status changes (auto-dismiss after 4 seconds)
   - Skeleton loading states for invoice index while data loads
   - Form auto-save to localStorage (restore on page refresh if unsaved changes)
   - Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+S to save draft, Ctrl+Enter to save & send (new invoices only)
   - Focus management: after adding line item, focus description field of new row

## Validation Rules (Reference Summary)
- Invoice: client_name (required, max 100), client_email (required, email format), due_date (required when sent, >= issue_date)
- LineItem: description (required, max 200), quantity (required, > 0), unit_price (required, >= 0)
- Status transitions: draft → sent only, sent → paid/overdue only, overdue → paid only

## Error Handling (Reference Summary)
- Form validation: inline field errors, preserve input on failure, disable submit during processing
- Status transitions: flash error messages for invalid transitions, button re-enabled on failure
- PDF generation: graceful failure with user-friendly error message, retry option
- Background job: log errors but continue processing remaining invoices

## UI/UX Notes
- Color scheme: status badges use semantic colors (success=green, warning=yellow, danger=red, info=blue)
- Typography: system font stack for performance, proper heading hierarchy
- Responsive breakpoints: mobile (<768px), tablet (768-1024px), desktop (>1024px)
- Accessibility: proper ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus indicators, color contrast compliance

## Assumptions
- Single-user application (no authentication required for v1)
- USD currency only, formatted with $ symbol and 2 decimal places
- Tax rate is uniform per invoice (not per line item)
- Invoice numbers auto-increment globally (INV-0001, INV-0002, etc.)
- PDF generation happens synchronously (acceptable for typical invoice sizes)
- Overdue detection runs daily at 6 AM server time
- No email delivery in v1 (PDF download only)

## Out of Scope
- Multi-user authentication and authorization
- Email delivery of invoices
- Payment gateway integration
- Recurring invoice templates
- Multiple currencies
- Advanced PDF customization (logos, custom layouts)
- Invoice approval workflows

## Future Considerations
- Email delivery: integrate with SendGrid/Postmark to email PDFs automatically when sent
- Payment integration: Stripe/PayPal links in PDFs for online payment
- Client portal: public links for clients to view and pay invoices
- Advanced PDF customization: company logos, custom color schemes, multiple templates
- Recurring invoices: template system with automated generation
- Multi-currency support with exchange rate handling
- Invoice approval workflow for team environments
- Advanced reporting: revenue analytics, aging reports, client payment history

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 5/5
- Specificity: 5/5
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 5/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Initial data model missing tax fields that totals calculation referenced — added tax_rate and tax_amount
- Status transition validations needed AASM-specific implementation details — specified transition methods and callbacks
- PDF generation error handling was vague — added specific rescue patterns and user feedback
- Line item positioning for ordering was missing — added position field with auto-assignment
- Mobile responsiveness mentioned but not specified — added concrete breakpoints and layout changes

**Remaining considerations:**
- Should tax rate be configurable per invoice or application-wide default?
- Does the user want email notifications when invoices become overdue?
- Should there be a "void" or "cancelled" status in addition to the current workflow?

**Questions for Consideration:**
1. Do you need email delivery when invoices are sent, or is PDF download sufficient for v1?
2. Should the tax rate be editable per invoice, or would you prefer a global default setting?
3. Would you like any additional invoice statuses (like "void" or "cancelled")?
4. Do you need any reporting features (revenue summaries, overdue reports) in the initial build?
5. Should clients be able to view their invoices via a public link, or is this purely for internal use?

# Stripe-Connected SaaS Platform

## Overview
A multi-tenant SaaS platform where users can subscribe to monthly plans and connect their own Stripe accounts to manage billing and payments. Each user gets their own workspace with subscription management and Stripe integration capabilities.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Multi-tenant data isolation (every query scoped by tenant/user)
- Stripe Connect OAuth flow for user account linking
- Webhook handling for subscription lifecycle events (payment success/failure, cancellation)
- Plan-based feature gating and access control

**Polish requirements:**
- Usage analytics dashboard, billing history exports
- Email notifications for payment events, advanced plan comparison UI

**Judgment calls:**
- Stripe Connect type: using Express accounts (simpler onboarding) vs Standard accounts (more control)
- Trial period handling: immediate access vs delayed billing
- Failed payment retry: automatic dunning vs manual intervention

## Tech Stack
Next.js 14 with TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Prisma ORM, NextAuth.js for authentication, Tailwind CSS.
Stripe integration: @stripe/stripe-js client-side, stripe server-side SDK.
Webhook handling: Next.js API routes with signature verification.

## Data Model

### User
- id: uuid (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required, valid email format)
- name: string (required, max 100)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_one Subscription, has_one StripeAccount

### Subscription
- id: uuid (primary key)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- plan_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- stripe_subscription_id: string (unique, required) — Stripe's subscription ID
- status: enum [trialing, active, past_due, canceled, unpaid] (required)
- current_period_start: timestamp (required)
- current_period_end: timestamp (required)
- trial_end: timestamp (nullable) — when trial expires
- canceled_at: timestamp (nullable)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User, belongs_to Plan

### Plan
- id: uuid (primary key)
- name: string (required, max 50) — display name (e.g., "Pro", "Enterprise")
- stripe_price_id: string (unique, required) — Stripe's price ID
- amount: integer (required, >= 0) — price in cents
- currency: string (required, default: "usd")
- interval: enum [month] (required) — billing frequency
- features: jsonb (required) — feature flags and limits
- active: boolean (default: true) — whether plan is available for new subscriptions
- created_at: timestamp

### StripeAccount
- id: uuid (primary key)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, unique, required)
- stripe_account_id: string (unique, required) — Stripe Connect account ID
- access_token: string (encrypted, required) — OAuth access token
- refresh_token: string (encrypted, nullable) — OAuth refresh token
- scope: string (required) — granted permissions
- connected_at: timestamp (required)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User

## User Flows

### User Registration & Plan Selection
1. User visits landing page → clicks "Get Started" → sees plan comparison (Free, Pro $29/mo, Enterprise $99/mo)
2. Selects plan → registration form (name, email, password) with plan pre-selected
3. Submits form → validation (email format, password strength 8+ chars) → creates User record
4. Redirects to Stripe Checkout with selected plan → user enters payment details
5. On successful payment → Stripe webhook creates Subscription record → user redirected to dashboard
6. On payment failure → redirected to retry page with error message

### Stripe Account Connection
1. From dashboard, user clicks "Connect Stripe Account" → sees explanation of benefits
2. Clicks "Connect" → redirects to Stripe Connect OAuth (Express account type)
3. User completes Stripe onboarding → Stripe redirects back with authorization code
4. App exchanges code for access token → creates StripeAccount record → shows success message
5. Dashboard now shows "Stripe Connected" status with account details

### Subscription Management
1. User navigates to "Billing" page → sees current plan, next billing date, payment method
2. To upgrade: clicks "Change Plan" → plan comparison with current plan highlighted → selects new plan
3. Stripe immediately prorates and charges difference → webhook updates Subscription record
4. To cancel: clicks "Cancel Subscription" → confirmation modal "Cancel at end of current period?"
5. On confirm → Stripe cancels at period end → webhook updates canceled_at field → shows "Cancels on [date]"

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User authentication system**
   - NextAuth.js setup with email/password provider
   - Registration form: name (required, max 100), email (required, unique, format validation), password (required, min 8 chars, strength indicator)
   - Login form with "Remember me" checkbox, password reset flow via email
   - Protected route middleware: redirect to login if unauthenticated
   - Validation: client-side with react-hook-form, server-side with zod schemas
   - Error handling: inline field errors, form-level error banner, loading states on submit buttons

2. **Plan management system**
   - Seed database with 3 plans: Free ($0), Pro ($29), Enterprise ($99)
   - Plan comparison page: feature matrix, highlight differences, "Current Plan" badge for logged-in users
   - Admin interface (simple form) to create/edit plans: name, Stripe price ID, amount, features JSON
   - Feature flags system: check user's plan.features in middleware for protected routes

### Phase 2: Core Billing
3. **Stripe Checkout integration**
   - Checkout session creation: POST /api/stripe/create-checkout with plan_id
   - Success page: /billing/success with session_id parameter → retrieve session → create Subscription record
   - Cancel page: /billing/cancel with retry button
   - Webhook endpoint: POST /api/webhooks/stripe with signature verification
   - Handle events: checkout.session.completed, invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted
   - Error handling: webhook failures logged, retried by Stripe automatically

4. **Subscription lifecycle management**
   - Dashboard subscription widget: current plan name, status badge (color-coded), next billing date, amount
   - Plan upgrade flow: immediate proration via Stripe API, webhook updates local record
   - Cancellation flow: cancel at period end (not immediate), show "Cancels on [date]" until period ends
   - Reactivation: if canceled but still in current period, allow "Resume Subscription" button
   - Failed payment handling: webhook sets status to past_due, show payment retry banner in dashboard

### Phase 3: Stripe Connect
5. **Stripe Connect OAuth integration**
   - Connect button: redirects to Stripe OAuth with state parameter for CSRF protection
   - OAuth callback: /api/stripe/connect/callback → exchange code for tokens → create StripeAccount record
   - Token encryption: use Next.js built-in crypto for access_token storage
   - Connection status: dashboard shows "Connected" badge with Stripe account ID, "Disconnect" option
   - Disconnect flow: revoke tokens via Stripe API, delete StripeAccount record, confirmation modal

6. **Connected account management**
   - Account details page: shows connected Stripe account info, connection date, granted permissions
   - Token refresh: background job to refresh expired tokens using refresh_token
   - Error handling: if API calls fail due to revoked access, show "Reconnect Required" banner
   - Webhook forwarding: option to forward relevant webhooks to user's connected account

## Assumptions
- Using Stripe Connect Express accounts (simpler than Standard, sufficient for most SaaS use cases)
- Single currency (USD) for all plans and billing
- Monthly billing only (no annual plans in v1)
- Free plan has no Stripe subscription (local-only user record)
- Trial period: 14 days for paid plans, starts immediately on signup
- Failed payment retry: Stripe's default dunning management (3 attempts over 3 weeks)
- Multi-tenant by user (not team/organization model)
- No usage-based billing (flat monthly rates only)

## Out of Scope
- Team/organization accounts with multiple users
- Annual billing or custom billing cycles
- Usage-based or metered billing
- Multiple currencies or international tax handling
- Advanced analytics or reporting dashboard
- White-label or custom branding options
- API access for third-party integrations

## Future Considerations
- **Team accounts**: organization model with multiple users, role-based permissions, team billing
- **Usage analytics**: track feature usage, generate insights, usage-based plan recommendations
- **Advanced billing**: annual discounts, custom enterprise pricing, invoice-based billing
- **Internationalization**: multiple currencies, VAT/tax handling, localized pricing
- **API platform**: REST/GraphQL API for third-party integrations, API key management
- **Advanced Connect features**: marketplace functionality, platform fees, multi-party payments

---

## Key Questions for Clarification

Before I finalize this spec, I'd like to clarify a few high-impact decisions:

1. **Stripe Connect scope**: What will users do with their connected Stripe accounts? Just view data, or do they need to create charges, manage customers, etc.? This affects the OAuth permissions requested.

2. **Free plan handling**: Should the Free plan create a Stripe customer record, or keep it entirely local? This affects the upgrade flow complexity.

3. **Trial period**: Do you want trials for all paid plans, or immediate billing? Should trials require a payment method upfront?

4. **Feature gating**: What specific features should be locked behind paid plans? This affects the middleware and UI conditional rendering.

5. **Multi-tenancy level**: Is this per-user (each user gets their own workspace), or do you want team/organization accounts where multiple users share a subscription?

---

# Stripe-Connected SaaS Platform

## Overview
A multi-tenant SaaS platform with three subscription tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) where users can manage projects, connect their Stripe accounts, and handle billing with instant plan changes and email receipts.

## Builder Notes
**Load-bearing requirements:**
- Project limit enforcement (3 for Free, unlimited for Pro/Enterprise)
- Instant plan changes with Stripe proration handling
- Multi-tenant data isolation (every query scoped by user_id)
- Webhook handling for subscription events and receipt email triggers

**Polish requirements:**
- Usage analytics, billing history exports, team invitation UI polish
- Advanced project templates, bulk project operations

**Judgment calls:**
- Team features scope: assuming basic team member invites + shared projects for Enterprise
- Downgrade handling: preserve excess projects as read-only vs. require deletion
- Email service: using Resend for transactional emails (modern, reliable)

## Tech Stack
Next.js 14 with TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Prisma ORM, NextAuth.js for authentication, Tailwind CSS.
Stripe integration: @stripe/stripe-js client-side, stripe server-side SDK.
Email: Resend for transactional emails (receipts, notifications).
Background jobs: Vercel Cron for webhook processing and email sending.

## Data Model

### User
- id: uuid (primary key)
- email: string (unique, required, valid email format)
- name: string (required, max 100)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: has_one Subscription, has_one StripeAccount, has_many Projects, has_many TeamMemberships

### Subscription
- id: uuid (primary key)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- plan_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- stripe_subscription_id: string (unique, nullable) — null for Free plan
- stripe_customer_id: string (unique, nullable) — null for Free plan
- status: enum [active, past_due, canceled, unpaid] (default: active)
- current_period_start: timestamp (nullable) — null for Free plan
- current_period_end: timestamp (nullable) — null for Free plan
- canceled_at: timestamp (nullable)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User, belongs_to Plan

### Plan
- id: uuid (primary key)
- name: string (required, max 50) — "Free", "Pro", "Enterprise"
- stripe_price_id: string (unique, nullable) — null for Free plan
- amount: integer (required, >= 0) — price in cents (0, 2900, 9900)
- currency: string (required, default: "usd")
- interval: enum [month] (required)
- project_limit: integer (nullable) — 3 for Free, null for unlimited
- team_features: boolean (default: false) — true only for Enterprise
- active: boolean (default: true)
- created_at: timestamp

### Project
- id: uuid (primary key)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, required) — project owner
- team_id: uuid (foreign key, nullable) — null for personal projects
- name: string (required, max 100)
- description: text (nullable)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User, belongs_to Team (optional)

### Team (Enterprise only)
- id: uuid (primary key)
- owner_id: uuid (foreign key, required) — Enterprise subscriber
- name: string (required, max 100)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User (owner), has_many TeamMemberships, has_many Projects

### TeamMembership (Enterprise only)
- id: uuid (primary key)
- team_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, required)
- role: enum [member, admin] (default: member)
- invited_at: timestamp (required)
- joined_at: timestamp (nullable) — null until invitation accepted
- Relationships: belongs_to Team, belongs_to User

### StripeAccount
- id: uuid (primary key)
- user_id: uuid (foreign key, unique, required)
- stripe_account_id: string (unique, required)
- access_token: string (encrypted, required)
- refresh_token: string (encrypted, nullable)
- scope: string (required)
- connected_at: timestamp (required)
- created_at: timestamp
- Relationships: belongs_to User

## User Flows

### Registration & Plan Selection
1. User visits landing page → sees plan comparison: Free (3 projects), Pro ($29/mo, unlimited), Enterprise ($99/mo, unlimited + teams)
2. Clicks plan → registration form (name, email, password) with selected plan highlighted
3. For Free: creates User + Subscription (no Stripe) → redirects to dashboard
4. For Pro/Enterprise: creates User → redirects to Stripe Checkout with plan pre-selected
5. Stripe Checkout success → webhook creates Subscription with stripe_customer_id → redirects to dashboard
6. Checkout canceled → redirects to plan selection with "Complete your signup" message

### Project Management with Limits
1. Dashboard shows "Projects (2/3)" for Free users, "Projects (15)" for Pro/Enterprise
2. Create project: if at limit, show upgrade prompt instead of create form
3. Free user at limit tries to create → modal: "Upgrade to Pro for unlimited projects" with upgrade button
4. Project creation form: name (required, max 100), description (optional), team assignment (Enterprise only)
5. Project list: sortable by name/date, search filter, delete with confirmation modal

### Plan Upgrades/Downgrades
1. Billing page shows current plan with "Change Plan" button → plan comparison with current highlighted
2. Upgrade (Free → Pro/Enterprise): redirects to Stripe Checkout → webhook updates subscription
3. Upgrade (Pro → Enterprise): immediate proration via Stripe API → webhook updates → shows "Upgraded!" toast
4. Downgrade (Pro/Enterprise → Free): confirmation modal "You'll be limited to 3 projects. Continue?"
5. If user has >3 projects on downgrade → shows project selection: "Choose 3 projects to keep active"
6. Excess projects marked as archived (read-only) until user upgrades again or deletes them

### Team Features (Enterprise Only)
1. Enterprise dashboard shows "Teams" tab → create team form (name required)
2. Team page: member list, invite form (email input), shared projects section
3. Invite flow: email sent with join link → recipient clicks → creates account or logs in → joins team
4. Team projects: visible to all members, editable by admins and project creator
5. Team member management: owner can promote to admin, remove members, transfer ownership

### Email Receipts
1. Stripe webhook invoice.payment_succeeded → queues email job with invoice data
2. Email template: company header, invoice details (plan, period, amount), payment method, PDF attachment
3. Send via Resend API → log delivery status → retry on failure (3 attempts)

## Features (Implementation Order)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. **User authentication & basic project management**
   - NextAuth.js setup with email/password provider, registration/login forms
   - User model with validation: email (unique, format), name (required, max 100), password (min 8 chars)
   - Project CRUD: create form (name required max 100, description optional), list view with search
   - Project limit middleware: check user's plan.project_limit before allowing creation
   - Error handling: inline validation errors, loading states, success toasts
   - Dashboard: project count display "Projects (X/3)" or "Projects (X)" for unlimited

2. **Plan system & Free tier**
   - Seed 3 plans: Free (0, limit 3), Pro (2900, unlimited), Enterprise (9900, unlimited + teams)
   - Plan comparison page: feature matrix, pricing, "Current Plan" badge for logged-in users
   - Free tier signup: creates User + Subscription (no Stripe records) → immediate dashboard access
   - Project limit enforcement: disable "New Project" button when at limit, show upgrade prompt modal

### Phase 2: Paid Subscriptions
3. **Stripe Checkout integration**
   - Checkout session creation: POST /api/stripe/create-checkout with plan_id and user_id
   - Success webhook: checkout.session.completed → create/update Subscription with stripe_customer_id and stripe_subscription_id
   - Billing page: current plan display, next billing date, payment method from Stripe
   - Plan upgrade from Free: redirect to Checkout → webhook creates paid subscription → unlimited projects unlocked
   - Error handling: checkout failures redirect to retry page, webhook failures logged and retried

4. **Plan changes & proration**
   - Change plan flow: billing page → "Change Plan" → comparison with current highlighted → confirm selection
   - Pro ↔ Enterprise changes: immediate via Stripe API subscription update with proration
   - Downgrade to Free: confirmation modal → cancel Stripe subscription → webhook updates to Free plan
   - Excess project handling: if >3 projects on Free downgrade → project selection UI → archive excess as read-only
   - Success feedback: toast notifications for all plan changes, updated billing page display

### Phase 3: Advanced Features
5. **Email receipts system**
   - Resend integration: API key in environment variables, email templates in /emails directory
   - Webhook handler: invoice.payment_succeeded → extract invoice data → queue email job
   - Receipt email template: company branding, invoice details (plan, billing period, amount), payment method last 4 digits
   - Email queue processing: background job sends via Resend → logs success/failure → retries failed sends (max 3 attempts)
   - User preference: opt-out checkbox in billing settings (default: enabled)

6. **Team features (Enterprise only)**
   - Team model and UI: Enterprise users see "Teams" tab → create team form → team dashboard
   - Team invitation system: invite form (email input) → sends email with join link → recipient signup/login → auto-joins team
   - Team project sharing: project creation form shows team dropdown (Enterprise only) → team members can view/edit
   - Team member management: owner/admin roles, member list with role badges, promote/demote/remove actions
   - Team project permissions: all members can view, admins + project creator can edit/delete

### Phase 4: Stripe Connect
7. **Stripe Connect OAuth integration**
   - Connect flow: dashboard "Connect Stripe" button → OAuth redirect → callback handler → store encrypted tokens
   - Account management: connection status display, account details page, disconnect option with confirmation
   - Token refresh: background job refreshes expired tokens using refresh_token
   - Error handling: revoked access detection → "Reconnect Required" banner → re-auth flow

## Validation Rules
- User email: valid format, unique across users, max 255 chars
- User name: required, max 100 chars, min 2 chars
- User password: min 8 chars, at least 1 number, 1 uppercase (registration only)
- Project name: required, max 100 chars, unique per user
- Project description: optional, max 500 chars
- Team name: required, max 100 chars, unique per owner
- Team member email: valid format, not already a team member

## Error Handling
- Form validation: client-side with react-hook-form + zod, server-side validation with Prisma constraints
- Stripe API errors: network failures show retry button, card declined shows specific message, expired checkout redirects to new session
- Webhook failures: logged with full payload, Stripe auto-retries, manual retry endpoint for debugging
- Email delivery failures: logged, retried up to 3 times, user notified if all attempts fail
- Project limit exceeded: friendly modal with upgrade CTA, disabled create button with tooltip
- Team invitation errors: invalid email shows inline error, already invited shows "Invitation pending"

## UI/UX Notes
- Responsive design: mobile-first with Tailwind breakpoints, collapsible sidebar on mobile
- Loading states: skeleton screens for project lists, spinner buttons during form submission, progress bars for file uploads
- Empty states: "No projects yet" with create button, "No team members" with invite prompt
- Plan comparison: highlight differences, "Most Popular" badge on Pro, feature checkmarks/X marks
- Billing page: clear next billing date, payment method with last 4 digits, change/cancel options
- Team UI: member avatars, role badges, clear invite/remove actions
- Toast notifications: success (green, auto-dismiss 3s), error (red, manual dismiss), info (blue, auto-dismiss 5s)

## Assumptions
- Credit card only (no ACH, wire transfers, or alternative payment methods)
- USD currency only for all plans and billing
- Monthly billing cycle only (no annual discounts in v1)
- Team features limited to project sharing and member management (no advanced permissions, audit logs, etc.)
- Downgrade excess projects become read-only (not deleted) to allow easy re-upgrade
- Email receipts sent immediately after successful payment (not batched)
- Stripe Connect permissions: read_write scope for full account access
- Free plan users can connect Stripe accounts (no plan restriction on Connect feature)

## Out of Scope
- Annual billing or custom billing cycles
- Usage-based billing or metered features
- Advanced team permissions (project-level roles, approval workflows)
- Multi-currency support or international tax handling
- Invoice-based billing for Enterprise
- Advanced analytics or usage reporting
- API access for third-party integrations
- White-label or custom branding options

## Future Considerations
- **Annual billing**: 20% discount for annual plans, proration handling for mid-cycle changes
- **Advanced team features**: project-level permissions, approval workflows, audit logs, team analytics
- **Usage analytics**: track project creation, feature usage, generate insights for plan recommendations
- **Enterprise sales**: custom pricing, invoice billing, dedicated support, SSO integration
- **API platform**: REST API for project management, webhook subscriptions, rate limiting
- **Internationalization**: multiple currencies, VAT handling, localized pricing and emails
- **Advanced billing**: dunning management, failed payment recovery, custom retry schedules

## Self-Assessment
**Ratings** (1-5):
- Completeness: 5/5
- Specificity: 5/5
- Consistency: 5/5
- Proportionality: 4/5
- Translation Quality: 5/5
- Implementation Coverage: 5/5

**Issues caught and fixed:**
- Data model initially missing team_id foreign key on Projects table — added for Enterprise team project sharing
- Plan change flow didn't specify how to handle users with >3 projects downgrading to Free — added project selection UI and read-only archiving
- Email receipt system was missing user opt-out preference — added to billing settings
- Team invitation flow was missing the actual email sending mechanism — specified Resend integration and email template

**Remaining considerations:**
- Should archived projects (from Free downgrades) have a time limit before deletion, or remain indefinitely?
- Do you want team members to have individual billing, or is team billing always through the Enterprise subscriber?
- Should Stripe Connect integration be available to Free users, or restricted to paid plans?
